title: The CropSync Harvest Crisis: Database Full

narrator.personality: DevOps engineer who grew up on a farm. Practical, no-nonsense. Keeps relating the urgency to actual farmers who depend on the platform. Says things like 'we are losing daylight here.'

story_beats.message: Three more counties just reported the app is down. Farmers are calling our support line directly -- they say they cannot log irrigation schedules and the forecast shows rain in 6 hours. If they miss this window, crops dry out.

CEO just got off the phone with our biggest customer, a 2,000-acre corn operation in Iowa. They are threatening to switch to a competitor if we cannot get this fixed before the rain window. We are losing daylight here.
The EC2 instances are healthy and the application code has not changed. The issue is at the database layer. Take a closer look at the RDS instance metrics.

hints: The application is timing out on database writes. What could cause a database to reject write operations?
Check the RDS instance metrics in CloudWatch. Storage-related metrics can tell you a lot about database health.
The FreeStorageSpace metric is at zero. The RDS instance has run out of disk space, which causes all write operations to fail.
You need to increase the allocated storage for the RDS instance. RDS supports storage auto-scaling -- that would prevent this from happening again.

resolution.root_cause: The RDS MySQL instance cropsync-prod-db (db.t3.medium, 20GB allocated storage) ran out of disk space. A runaway logging query and accumulated binary logs consumed all available storage, causing all INSERT and UPDATE operations to fail with 'The table is full' errors.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the RDS instance has zero free storage space
Player proposes increasing the allocated storage or enabling storage auto-scaling on the RDS instance
Player identifies that binary logs or accumulated data caused the storage to fill up

learning_objectives: RDS instances have a fixed storage allocation that can be exhausted by data growth, logs, or temporary files
CloudWatch FreeStorageSpace metric is critical for monitoring RDS health and should always have an alarm
RDS storage auto-scaling can automatically increase storage when free space drops below a threshold
